name: Forter E-commerce Fraud Prevention API
what_it_does: Forter e-commerce fraud prevention REST API for merchants and platforms to get real-time approve/decline fraud decisions on transactions, account takeovers, promotion abuse, and policy violations backed by Forter's identity trust network and chargeback guarantee, covering the full customer journey from account creation through checkout and post-purchase returns. Enables AI agents to manage transaction fraud decisioning for e-commerce checkout automation, handle account creation risk scoring for registration fraud automation, access account takeover detection for login security automation, retrieve promotion abuse detection for coupon and discount fraud automation, manage return fraud detection for post-purchase policy automation, handle payment method intelligence for card fraud automation, access identity trust score for customer risk profiling automation, retrieve fraud decision explanation for dispute and review automation, manage chargeback dispute documentation for fraud guarantee automation, and integrate Forter with Shopify, Magento, Salesforce Commerce, and enterprise e-commerce platforms for end-to-end fraud prevention automation.
use_cases: Getting real-time approve/decline decisions from e-commerce checkout fraud agents
Detecting account takeover at login from identity security agents
Preventing promotion and coupon abuse from discount fraud detection agents
Handling chargeback guarantee claims from fraud prevention automation agents
not_for: Bank card fraud detection at network level (use Mastercard/Visa fraud APIs for issuer-side)
General identity verification/KYC (use Jumio or Onfido for ID document verification)
Crypto transaction fraud (Forter focuses on e-commerce not crypto)
best_when: An e-commerce merchant or platform wanting AI agents to automate real-time fraud prevention across the full customer journey with identity trust decisioning and chargeback guarantee through Forter's machine learning fraud network.
avoid_when: DECISION IS APPROVE/DECLINE — NOT A SCORE: Forter returns approve/decline/not_sure decision not a fraud score; automated integration must handle three-way decision not binary score comparison; automated score-based threshold logic for Forter creates misaligned decisioning. FORTER JAVASCRIPT TAG REQUIRED FOR FULL SIGNAL: Forter fraud accuracy depends on JavaScript tag on merchant site for device fingerprint and behavioral signals; automated decision quality without JS tag is reduced; automated server-side-only Forter integration creates degraded accuracy with higher decline rate. CHARGEBACK GUARANTEE REQUIRES FORTER APPROVE: Forter only guarantees chargebacks for transactions approved by Forter; automated merchant override of Forter decline creates unguaranteed chargeback liability; automated full chargeback guarantee assumption after override creates unexpected financial exposure. NOT_SURE REQUIRES MERCHANT DECISION: Forter not_sure response requires merchant to decide based on own risk tolerance; automated workflow must define not_sure handling policy; automated treating not_sure as approve creates higher fraud on uncertain orders.

auth.notes: Forter uses API key + HMAC signature for authentication. REST API with JSON. New York/Tel Aviv HQ. Founded 2013 by Michael Reitblat and Alon Shemesh. Backed by Goldman Sachs, Tiger Global, Sequoia ($525M+ raised, $3B+ valuation). Products: Transaction fraud, account protection, promo abuse, return fraud, chargeback guarantee. SDKs: JS, Python, Java, PHP. PCI DSS. SOC2. GDPR. Chargeback guarantee covers approved transactions. Competes with Riskified and Kount.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise sales and pilot required
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-transaction pricing negotiated; typically percentage of GMV (0.1-0.5% of transaction value); chargeback guarantee included
pricing.notes: New York/Tel Aviv. Goldman/Tiger backed. Per-transaction % of GMV. Enterprise contract with chargeback guarantee.

performance.rate_limits: High throughput per merchant account; decision APIs have high rate limits; webhooks for chargeback and dispute events

known_agent_gotchas: THREE-WAY DECISION NOT BINARY: Forter returns approve/decline/not_sure — not a score; automated decisioning must explicitly handle not_sure policy (approve, decline, or manual review); automated binary approve/decline mapping creates undefined not_sure handling and potential fraud exposure

JAVASCRIPT TAG REQUIRED FOR ACCURACY: Forter's JS tag on merchant site provides device fingerprint and behavioral signals critical to decision accuracy; automated server-side-only integration without JS tag creates 15-20% worse fraud detection; automated full-accuracy assumption without JS tag creates higher fraud and false declines

CHARGEBACK GUARANTEE ONLY ON FORTER APPROVE: Forter financial guarantee covers only transactions Forter returned approve for; automated merchant override of decline loses guarantee coverage; automated chargeback guarantee assumption after override creates uninsured chargeback liability

ORDER UPDATE ENDPOINT FOR POST-AUTH EVENTS: Forter requires order update API calls after fulfillment, return, and cancellation events to maintain model accuracy; automated post-purchase pipeline must send updates; automated fire-and-forget after decision creates degraded model accuracy over time

HMAC SIGNATURE ON WEBHOOK: Forter webhooks (chargeback events) signed with HMAC; automated webhook processor must verify signature; automated unverified webhook creates spoofed chargeback claim acceptance
agent_readiness.security_notes: E-commerce fraud prevention. PCI-DSS, SOC2, GDPR. API key + HMAC. US/EU. Transaction and identity trust data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Fraud prevention. PCI-DSS, SOC2. API key + HMAC. US/EU. Transaction and behavioral data.
